title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Best Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home. This creamy, rich, and easy recipe is perfect for lunch or dinner. Get the full step-by-step guide!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Authentic Indian Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. Perfect for a satisfying lunch!

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish featuring tender chicken pieces swimming in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This beloved recipe balances sweet and savory notes with aromatic spices, offering a truly comforting and indulgent meal. It is a perfect choice for a flavorful lunch that satisfies every palate.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1/2 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Saute for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Add the tomato puree, soaked cashews (drained), and remaining 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder to the pan. Stir well. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and oil begins to separate.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra smooth texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan, pressing down on any solids. Discard any remaining solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken, remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, sugar, and crushed Kasoori Methi. Stir well. Pour in the heavy cream and gently simmer for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Adjust salt and sugar to taste.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice for a truly authentic Indian experience.

tips: For maximum flavor and tenderness, marinate the chicken for at least 2 hours or overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the spices and yogurt to deeply penetrate the meat.

To achieve an incredibly smooth and restaurant-quality sauce, make sure to blend the tomato-cashew mixture until no lumps remain. Passing it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending is a pro trick for ultimate silkiness.
Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) is a signature ingredient for authentic Butter Chicken. Crush it between your palms just before adding to release its aromatic bitterness and depth of flavor.
article:
Butter Chicken, known traditionally as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global phenomenon, a culinary hug in a bowl that has captivated palates worldwide. Originating from the bustling streets of Delhi, this iconic Indian lunch recipe has become a staple in homes and restaurants, celebrated for its rich, creamy tomato gravy and succulent pieces of marinated chicken. If you've ever yearned to recreate that authentic, restaurant-quality taste in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This article will guide you through the journey of mastering the art of Butter Chicken, ensuring a delicious and satisfying meal every time.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

At its heart, Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is a mild, creamy, and subtly sweet curry that balances a medley of spices with the tang of tomatoes and the richness of butter and cream. Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, where cooks ingeniously combined leftover tandoori chicken with a rich tomato gravy, creating a dish that quickly rose to fame. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," aptly describing the luscious foundation of this beloved curry. Unlike many fiery Indian dishes, Butter Chicken offers a milder flavor profile, making it incredibly approachable and a favorite among those new to Indian cuisine, while still satisfying seasoned connoisseurs with its depth of flavor. It’s the ultimate comfort food, perfect for an indulgent Indian lunch recipe.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients, each playing a vital role in achieving that signature flavor and texture.

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   **Chicken:** 1.5 lbs (about 700g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces. Thighs are recommended for juiciness.
*   **Plain Yogurt:** ½ cup, full-fat for tenderness.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (for color, less heat), ½ teaspoon turmeric powder, ½ teaspoon garam masala, ½ teaspoon cumin powder, salt to taste.

**For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:**
*   **Butter:** 4 tablespoons (unsalted), plus more for finishing.
*   **Oil:** 1 tablespoon (neutral oil like vegetable or canola).
*   **Onions:** 1 large, finely chopped or pureed.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, pureed.
*   **Cashews:** ¼ cup, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes (for richness and thickness).
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, ½ teaspoon turmeric powder.
*   **Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** 1 tablespoon, crushed between your palms – this is crucial for authentic flavor.
*   **Heavy Cream:** ½ cup, or more to taste.
*   **Sugar or Honey:** 1-2 teaspoons, to balance the acidity of the tomatoes.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Garnish:** Fresh cilantro, chopped.

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe Instructions

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken that will impress even the most discerning palates.

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients (yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, and salt). Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor and tenderness.

**Step 2: Cook the Marinated Chicken**
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook in batches if necessary. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this stage, as it will simmer further in the sauce. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base**
In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped or pureed onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the crushed or pureed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and the dry spices (Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt). Mix well and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the mixture.

**Step 4: Blend and Strain the Sauce**
Remove the pan from the heat and let the sauce cool slightly. Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky, restaurant-quality sauce, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid. Discard any remaining solids.

**Step 5: Finish the Butter Chicken Curry**
Return the sieved sauce to the pot and place it over medium-low heat. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, the kasuri methi (crushed between your palms), and the cooked chicken pieces. Stir gently to combine. Pour in the heavy cream and add the sugar or honey. Simmer the Butter Chicken for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt or sugar as needed.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that perfect, authentic Butter Chicken isn't just about following the steps; a few professional tips can elevate your dish from good to unforgettable.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The yogurt and spices tenderize the chicken and infuse it with flavor, making a huge difference in the final taste and texture. Overnight marination yields the best results.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** For a truly luxurious, restaurant-style sauce, ensure you blend the sauce base until it's absolutely smooth. Straining it through a fine-mesh sieve afterward removes any tiny bits of onion or cashew, resulting in an incredibly velvety texture.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are non-negotiable for authentic Murgh Makhani flavor. Lightly toast them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Use good quality, full-fat dairy products (yogurt, cream, butter) for the richest flavor and texture. Kashmiri red chili powder is essential for that vibrant red color without excessive heat.
*   **Gentle Simmer:** Once you've added the cream, avoid boiling the curry vigorously, as it can cause the cream to split. A gentle simmer is all you need to warm through and meld the flavors.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a perfect centerpiece for any Indian lunch recipe.
Serve your luscious Murgh Makhani hot, garnished generously with fresh chopped cilantro and an optional swirl of extra cream. It's traditionally enjoyed with fluffy Basmati rice, which soaks up the rich sauce wonderfully. For a truly authentic experience, serve it alongside warm, buttery naan bread, roti, or paratha, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the delicious gravy. A simple side salad or a cooling cucumber raita can also provide a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is undeniably delicious, there are several ways to adapt this recipe to suit different dietary needs or preferences without losing its essence.

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** For a meat-free option, substitute the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas. Marinate and cook them similarly to the chicken, or simply add them directly to the simmering sauce to absorb the flavors.
*   **Dairy-Free Version:** To make it dairy-free, use a plant-based yogurt for marination and substitute heavy cream with full-fat coconut cream or cashew cream. Use a neutral oil or vegan butter in place of dairy butter.
*   **Adjusting Spice Levels:** If you prefer more heat, add a pinch of regular red chili powder or a couple of slit green chilies to the sauce base. For less heat, reduce the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder.
*   **Healthier Alternatives:** While Butter Chicken is an indulgent dish, you can make it slightly healthier by reducing the amount of butter and cream, or by using light coconut milk instead of heavy cream. Using boneless, skinless chicken breast can also reduce fat content.

Embrace the joy of cooking this iconic Indian lunch recipe at home. The rich aromas filling your kitchen and the incredible flavors on your plate will make the effort truly worthwhile. Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken!
